title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Bacon and Claddock discuss the possibility that witnesses are withholding information before being interrupted by Old Crickenshaw, the owner of the Elizabethan warehouse where the murder occurred. Crickenshaw speculates that the victim was a foreigner or a spy attempting to steal industrial secrets from a nearby metal box factory. He further suggests the woman was connected to his son, Alfred, claiming a brute killed her while she was attempting to meet him; however, Claddock counters this by noting that Alfred did not recognize the woman. After Crickenshaw departs, Bacon dismisses the theory regarding Alfred and suggests focusing on Brian Estridge, an aimless former Air Force veteran. Bacon also questions the logic of a killer burying the victim in a coffin within her own father-in-law's house.

Claddock then visits Miss Marple, explaining that he was assigned to the case after the Deputy Commissioner took an interest in her involvement. They discuss the investigation, which began after Mrs. McGillicuddy reported the crime. Miss Marple asserts that Mrs. McGillicuddy would not have hallucinated the event and posits that the killer must be at or very familiar with Larchfield Manor, which could include locals or former maids. Regarding the victim's identity, forensic evidence suggests she was a healthy woman of approximately thirty-five years, possibly married with a child. Her clothing indicates she traveled; her fur coat was purchased in London around Christmas Eve, while her other garments were from Paris. Police are currently checking missing persons reports in both cities.
